First, the function should take a single argument, which is the word. Then, it needs to determine if the length of that word is an odd number. 

So, the steps are: 
1. Get the length of the word. In Python, this can be done using the len() function.
2. Check if the length is odd. An odd number is when the length divided by 2 leaves a remainder of 1. So, using the modulo operator (%), if len(word) % 2 == 1, then it's odd.
